name: Pumpkin-Eggnog Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a 9-inch x 3-inch springform pan with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a food processor with the knife blade attached, blend gingersnaps, walnuts (optional), and 1/4 cup sugar until finely ground; stir in melted margarine.
4. Press the mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of the springform pan.
5. Set aside.
6. In a small bowl, beat egg whites with an electric mixer on high speed until foamy. Gradually beat in 3/4 cup sugar until soft peaks form; set aside.
7. In a large bowl, beat egg yolks with an electric mixer on medium speed with pumpkin, cream cheese, cinnamon, allspice, ginger, cloves, and 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg until well blended.
8. Fold the beaten egg whites into the cream-cheese mixture and pour over the crust in the springform pan.
9. Bake for 1 hour or until a knife inserted 1 inch from the edge of the cheesecake comes out clean.
10. Cool the cheesecake in the pan on a wire rack.
11. In a small saucepan, mix eggnog, cornstarch, and 1/8 teaspoon nutmeg. Heat over medium heat to boiling.
12. Reduce heat to low; simmer for 1 minute or until the mixture thickens. Let stand for 10 minutes to cool slightly.
13. Remove the side of the springform pan from the cheesecake and pour in the eggnog mixture.
14. Garnish with walnuts (optional).
15. Refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 4 hours, or until well chilled.
